fields.id: Stable local source id for notes and reasoning traces, generated from source title, URL, and blurb.
fields.title: Human-readable source title from the artifact ledger.
fields.source_type: Coarse type used for filtering and reliability expectations.
fields.lineage_tags: Branch/search tags inferred from title and blurb.
fields.evidence_role: How this source is currently being used.
fields.search_text: Concatenated title and blurb for simple full-text search.

sources.title: Doyle and Evelyn Zimmerman 50th anniversary announcement, Colby Free Press, 11 Jun 2004
sources.blurb: Family-supplied announcement establishes marriage on 14 Jun 1954 at Sacred Heart Catholic Church, Colby; documents Doyle in Selden until 1953, Evelyn in Winona until 1954, the couple meeting in January 1954, and the Hi-Plains Implement career from 1953 through retirement in June 1999. Contradicts the compiled 14 Jun 1953 marriage date.
sources.search_text: Doyle and Evelyn Zimmerman 50th anniversary announcement, Colby Free Press, 11 Jun 2004 Family-supplied announcement establishes marriage on 14 Jun 1954 at Sacred Heart Catholic Church, Colby; documents Doyle in Selden until 1953, Evelyn in Winona until 1954, the couple meeting in January 1954, and the Hi-Plains Implement career from 1953 through retirement in June 1999. Contradicts the compiled 14 Jun 1953 marriage date.

sources.title: Evelyn Delores Zimmerman obituary, Baalmann Mortuary
sources.blurb: Names Homer and Marjorie Clemans Mundell; states marriage to Doyle J. Zimmerman; says Evelyn was raised by grandmother Frances Adolph and graduated from Winona High School.
sources.search_text: Evelyn Delores Zimmerman obituary, Baalmann Mortuary Names Homer and Marjorie Clemans Mundell; states marriage to Doyle J. Zimmerman; says Evelyn was raised by grandmother Frances Adolph and graduated from Winona High School.

sources.title: Donald Lynn Mundell obituary, Fry Funeral Home
sources.blurb: Names Homer Claire Mundell in Donald's parent line and places Evelyn Zimmerman, Marilyn Ayer/Ayers, and Judy Bastian in the same sibling group.
sources.search_text: Donald Lynn Mundell obituary, Fry Funeral Home Names Homer Claire Mundell in Donald's parent line and places Evelyn Zimmerman, Marilyn Ayer/Ayers, and Judy Bastian in the same sibling group.

sources.title: Elizabeth Catherine Nauer Zimmerman, Find a Grave

sources.title: Selden Cemetery, KSGenWeb
sources.blurb: Lists Michael J. and Elizabeth A. Zimmerman on the same stone; also shows Nauer burials nearby. The same transcription lists Dorsey O. McClelland, Ora E. McClelland, and Almeda O. Dible on identical stones/same concrete base, making it an important Long/McClelland collateral-location source.

sources.title: The German Catholic Settlers of Waterloo County PDF
sources.blurb: Compiled Waterloo-area lead placing Thomas and Catharine Nauer in Waterloo County in 1871 and Cawker, Mitchell County, Kansas by 1880; also proposes Lorenz Nauer and Dorothea Meyer as possible parents for Thomas.
sources.search_text: The German Catholic Settlers of Waterloo County PDF Compiled Waterloo-area lead placing Thomas and Catharine Nauer in Waterloo County in 1871 and Cawker, Mitchell County, Kansas by 1880; also proposes Lorenz Nauer and Dorothea Meyer as possible parents for Thomas.

sources.title: Thomas A. Nauer, WikiTree
sources.blurb: Compiled lead naming Thomas as son of Johann Lorenz Nauer and Mary Dorothea Wand; use as a target, not proof, because the Waterloo PDF uses Dorothea Meyer, widow Wand/Wandt.
sources.search_text: Thomas A. Nauer, WikiTree Compiled lead naming Thomas as son of Johann Lorenz Nauer and Mary Dorothea Wand; use as a target, not proof, because the Waterloo PDF uses Dorothea Meyer, widow Wand/Wandt.

sources.title: New Prussia, Ontario locality context
sources.blurb: Wilmot Township settlement context for Rhenish Prussian Catholic families and later movement toward Bruce County; useful geographic context for the Nauer search, not a direct proof record.
sources.search_text: New Prussia, Ontario locality context Wilmot Township settlement context for Rhenish Prussian Catholic families and later movement toward Bruce County; useful geographic context for the Nauer search, not a direct proof record.

sources.title: Standesamt Lebehnke civil registration fond 55/589/0, Szukaj w Archiwach
sources.blurb: Urzad Stanu Cywilnego Lubianka, held at the Poznan State Archive branch in Pila: birth, marriage, and death registers 1875-1930, fully digitized and viewable online without login. Likely the only surviving Lebehnke vital records.
sources.search_text: Standesamt Lebehnke civil registration fond 55/589/0, Szukaj w Archiwach Urzad Stanu Cywilnego Lubianka, held at the Poznan State Archive branch in Pila: birth, marriage, and death registers 1875-1930, fully digitized and viewable online without login. Likely the only surviving Lebehnke vital records.

sources.title: Lebehnke Evangelical parish entry, westpreussen.de church-book survey
sources.blurb: States the parish was attached to Schneidemuehl, Kreis Kolmar, until becoming independent in 1839, and that the church books kept from 1846 onward were destroyed in the Second World War - making the civil registers above the primary target.
sources.search_text: Lebehnke Evangelical parish entry, westpreussen.de church-book survey States the parish was attached to Schneidemuehl, Kreis Kolmar, until becoming independent in 1839, and that the church books kept from 1846 onward were destroyed in the Second World War - making the civil registers above the primary target.

sources.title: Evelyn Delores Zimmerman obituary mirror, Legacy.com

sources.title: Evelyn Delores Mundell Zimmerman, Find a Grave
sources.blurb: Cemetery memorial, 1935-2023. Bio text copies the Baalmann obituary verbatim, including the erroneous June 14, 1953 marriage date, so it is not an independent witness for the marriage year.
sources.search_text: Evelyn Delores Mundell Zimmerman, Find a Grave Cemetery memorial, 1935-2023. Bio text copies the Baalmann obituary verbatim, including the erroneous June 14, 1953 marriage date, so it is not an independent witness for the marriage year.

sources.title: Walter William Mundell, FamilySearch
sources.blurb: Public index ties Walter to John Pryor Mundell and Sarah Alice Cantwell, gives Frances Rust as spouse with a 1910 marriage at Smith Center, Kansas, and lists children Homer Clair, Rex, and Erma.
sources.search_text: Walter William Mundell, FamilySearch Public index ties Walter to John Pryor Mundell and Sarah Alice Cantwell, gives Frances Rust as spouse with a 1910 marriage at Smith Center, Kansas, and lists children Homer Clair, Rex, and Erma.

sources.title: Sarah Alice Cantwell Mundell forum post, Genealogy.com archive

sources.title: Barnabas "Barney" Cantwell, FamilySearch
sources.blurb: Public index and snippets connect Sarah Alice Cantwell to the Barnabas Cantwell family and name Thomas Cantwell Jr. and Jemima Kelley as Barnabas's parents.

sources.title: Cantwell forum thread, Genealogy.com archive

sources.title: Mary Frances Rust forum post, Genealogy.com archive
sources.blurb: Gives the clue that Mary Frances Rust went by Frances, was born 6 Jun 1894 possibly in Smith County, Kansas, had a brother William/Bill Rust with wife Viola, divorced Walter William Mundell, married a man surnamed Adolph, then after his death married Lyle Strawn and lived in Beloit, Kansas.
sources.search_text: Mary Frances Rust forum post, Genealogy.com archive Gives the clue that Mary Frances Rust went by Frances, was born 6 Jun 1894 possibly in Smith County, Kansas, had a brother William/Bill Rust with wife Viola, divorced Walter William Mundell, married a man surnamed Adolph, then after his death married Lyle Strawn and lived in Beloit, Kansas.

sources.blurb: Search-result sibling list includes Mary Frances Rust Strawn, 1894-1984, and Clarence Aison Rust; useful for testing the William/Bill Rust brother clue.
sources.search_text: William Bryan Rust, Find a Grave Search-result sibling list includes Mary Frances Rust Strawn, 1894-1984, and Clarence Aison Rust; useful for testing the William/Bill Rust brother clue.

sources.title: OCCGS Harvey Mundell Civil War Veterans Project PDF
sources.blurb: Four-page compiled profile giving Harvey's April 1837 Clinton County, Indiana birth, parents John Mundell and Deborah Elmore, wife Elizabeth Jane Brown, children including John P. Mundell, Civil War service, and census/source trail.

sources.title: Southern Tuolumne County Historical Society genealogy page 213
sources.blurb: Compiled Harvey William Mundell page with John Mundell/Deborah Elmore parentage, Elizabeth Jane Brown marriage, Civil War service, and census trail; use as a pointer to primary records. Site verified July 2026 as the Southern Tuolumne County, California historical society, previously misattributed here to St. Charles County.

sources.title: Harvey William Mundell, FamilySearch
sources.blurb: Public index lead naming John Mundell and Deborah Elmore as parents. A July 2026 automated re-check found this profile ID resolving to an unrelated person, apparently after a tree merge; verify in a browser before relying on it.

sources.title: Henry Sleight, FamilySearch
sources.blurb: Public sibling-index result naming John Sleight and Elizabeth Gaunt as parents and listing John Gaunt Sleight among siblings. sources.title: Muhlenberg County Heritage, Vol. 3 No. 1
sources.blurb: Reprints the 1911 Muhlenberg Sentinel "Fords and Allens" letter by Rev. G. W. Ford, son of John Laborn Ford and Lucy Ann Staples; gives the Caswell-to-Greenville/Friendship migration, the Ford/Staples child list, John Burton Staples and Nancy Ann Turner details, and the family-tradition version of John Laborn Ford's parents.
